data.title: Portillo's shares could at least double in two years, activist Engaged Capital says
data.description:
Hot dog chain Portillo's (NASDAQ:PTLO) could see it shares at least double in two years with some improvements, activist Engaged Capital said on Tuesday,

Despite Portillo's having food at a great price and industry-leading average unit volumes, the restaurant operator trades at a substantial discount to growth peers, Engaged Capital highlighted in a slide deck it presented Tuesday at the 13D Active-Passive Investor Summit in New York.

Engaged argues that through a combination of improved same stores sales and revised development strategy that prioritizes on cash returns, Portillo’s current valuation multiple has the potential to re-rate from under 12x today into the high teens.

"We believe increased marketing investment, the current rollout of ordering kiosks, and a loyalty program will drive improvement in same restaurant sales," Engaged wrote in the deck. "We believe Portillo’s current valuation gives no credit for optimization of unit economic model or long duration unit growth, creating a highly attractive opportunity for current investors."

Engaged sees a scenario where Portillo's (PTLO) shares could rise to ~$26 or as high as ~$33 by December 2026.

The Engaged presentation comes after CNBC reported in August that Engaged had accumulated a nearly 10% stake and was pushing for changes. The activist is talking with management on improving operations, margins and branding as the Chicago-based chain is expanding nationally.

data.title: Seagate slips despite earnings, guidance surpassing estimates
data.description:
Seagate Technology (NASDAQ:STX) slipped 2% during early post-market trading Tuesday despite first quarter fiscal 2025 financial results and outlook surpassing estimates across the board.

Seagate reported adjusted earnings per share of $1.58, which was well above the consensus estimate of $1.46. Revenue for the quarter ended September 27 was $2.17B, versus the estimate of $2.13B. Seagate's current fiscal year runs from July 2024 through June 2025.

For the quarter in progress, Seagate forecasts adjusted earnings per share ranging from $1.65 to $2.05. With a midpoint of $1.85, it is well above the consensus estimate of $1.70. Seagate expects revenue ranging from $2.15B to $2.45B with a midpoint of $2.3B, which is again above the consensus of $2.28B.

The Fremont, Calif.-based data storage company also increased its quarterly cash dividend by 3% to $0.72 per share.

"Seagate is off to an outstanding start to the fiscal year, highlighted by gross margin expanding to the highest level in more than a decade," said CEO Dave Mosley.

"Our confidence in Seagate's future opportunities is reflected in the decision to raise the quarterly dividend as announced today," he added.

Seagate's gross margin of 32.9% for the first quarter was substantially stronger than the 10% reported for the same quarter last year.

What's more, Seagate reported a net income of $305M for the first quarter of fiscal 2025 versus a loss of $184M last year.

data.title: Starbucks falls after soft preliminary FQ4 results, suspended guidance
data.description:
Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) fell sharply in postmarket trading on Tuesday after reporting preliminary financial results for FQ4.

Global comparable store sales declined 7% during the quarter, and consolidated net revenue fell 3% to $9.1 billion, vs. $9.4 billion consensus. Non-GAAP EPS was reported at $0.80 vs $1.03 consensus and $1.06 a year ago.

Starbucks (SBUX) said the results were primarily driven by softness in North America’s revenues in the quarter, specifically a 6% decline in U.S. comparable store sales, driven by a 10% decline in comparable transactions, partially offset by a 4% increase in average ticket. The accelerated investments in an expanded range of product offerings coupled with more frequent in-app promotions and integrated marketing to entice frequency across the customer base did not improve customer behaviors, specifically traffic across both Starbucks Rewards and non-SR customer segments, resulting in lower-than-expected performance. Additionally, China comparable store sales declined 14%, driven by an 8% decline in average ticket compounded by a 6% decline in comparable transactions, weighed down by intensified competition and a soft macro environment that impacted consumer spending.

Due to the CEO transition and the current state of the business, Starbucks (SBUX) said guidance will be suspended for the full fiscal year 2025. This is anticipated to allow ample opportunity to complete an assessment of the business and solidify key strategies, while stabilizing and positioning the business for long-term growth.

On the capital allocation front, the Starbucks (SBUX) board approved an increase in the quarterly cash dividend from $0.57 to $0.61 per share of outstanding common stock.

Shares of Starbucks (SBUX) were down 4.95% in postmarket trading.

data.title: Agree Realty Q3 earnings, revenue beat as investment activity increases
data.description:
Agree Realty (NYSE:ADC) boosted the lower end of its full-year earnings guidance after Q3 earnings beat the average analyst estimate by a penny.

"We have accelerated investment activity while strengthening our balance sheet through proactive capital markets transactions," said President and CEO Joey Agree.

The retail REIT raised the lower end of its 2024 adjusted FFO per share guidance by a penny to $4.12 from $4.11 and kept the upper end of its range at $4.14. The midpoint of $4.13 compares with the consensus estimate of $4.10.

General and administrative expenses are expected to be 5.7%-5.9% of adjusted revenue, compared with its previous guidance of 5.7%-6.0%.

The company expects acquisition volume of ~$850M vs. its prior view of ~$700M. Disposition volume was changed to $70M-$100M from $60M-$100M previously.

Q3 adjusted FFO per share of $1.03, topping the $1.02 consensus estimate, slipped from $1.04 in Q2 and increased from $1.00 in last year's Q3.

Revenue of $154.3M, beating the $153.9M consensus, increased from $152.6M in the prior quarter and $136.8M a year ago.

Q3 operating expenses of $81.7M climbed from $77.8M in Q2 and $73.7M in Q3 2023.

As of Sept. 30, 2024, Agree's (ADC) portfolio consisted of 2,271 properties in 49 states and contained 47.2M square feet of gross leasable area. The portfolio was 99.6% leased at Sept. 30 vs. 99.8% at June 30, had a weighted average remaining lease term of 7.9 years, and generated 67.5% of annualized base rents from investment-grade retail tenants.

Its total acquisition volume for the quarter was $215.6M and included 66 properties net leased to retailers in sectors including general merchandise, warehouse clubs, home improvement, auto parts, tire and auto service, and grocery stores. It sold two properties for gross proceeds of $7.2M in Q3.

Conference call on Oct. 23 at 9:00 AM ET.

Agree Realty (ADC) stock was little changed at $75.11 in Tuesday after-hours trading.

Earlier, Agree Realty FFO of $1.03 beats by $0.01, revenue of $154.33M beats by $0.46M
